title: Trump Suggests Vance and Rubio Should Run Together in 2028 Election
description: President Trump said Vice President JD Vance and Secretary of State Marco Rubio should split a ticket, without mentioning who should be at the top.
author: Jonathan Wolfe

title: California Governor’s Race Remains Too Close to Call
description: Steve Hilton, a Republican, and Xavier Becerra, a Democrat, were leading in election night returns. Tom Steyer, another Democrat, urged patience as more votes were being counted.
author: Laurel Rosenhall

title: Ruth Shack, Early Champion of Gay Rights in Miami, Dies at 94
description: She sponsored one of the nation’s first anti-discrimination ordinances for gays and lesbians and later helped make Miami a global arts center.
author: Clay Risen

title: Knicks Fans Invade San Antonio, Chasing a Finals Rematch 27 Years in the Making
description: From Spike Lee to die-hard fans haunted by the 1999 finals, New Yorkers have descended on San Antonio in hopes of witnessing the franchise’s first championship in more than 50 years.
author: Edgar Sandoval and Sarah Maslin Nir

title: Out of Time to Delay, House to Vote on Trump’s Power to Continue War in Iran
description: A measure to direct the president to halt U.S. engagement in Iran had been on track to pass in late May, but Republican leaders postponed action. They have run out of time to delay the vote.
author: Robert Jimison and Megan Mineiro

title: U.S. Is Said to Be Investigating George Santos Over Kalshi Betting
description: The former congressman was referred to federal authorities after he bet on his own attendance at the State of the Union address, a person familiar with the matter said.
author: David Yaffe-Bellany and Sharon LaFraniere

title: Josh Turek, a Paralympian, Wins the Democratic Senate Primary in Iowa
description: Mr. Turek, a state lawmaker, beat Zach Wahls in a contest that tested the party’s anti-establishment fervor.
author: Lisa Lerer

title: Incumbent Karen Bass Advances to L.A. Mayoral Election in November
author: Shawn Hubler

title: Spencer Pratt Suggests He Can Be a Mayor for all L.A. Residents, as Vote Counting Continues
description: The former reality TV star has tried to broaden his platform, talking about Los Angeles’s homelessness and drugs problem.
author: Conor Dougherty

title: Here’s the latest.
author: Jill Cowan

title: Gregg Hull Wins G.O.P. Nomination to Be New Mexico Governor
description: Gregg Hull, the former mayor of Rio Rancho, a suburb of Albuquerque, will try to break the Democrats’ hold on the southwestern state.
author: Reis Thebault

title: Deb Haaland Wins Nomination in New Mexico Governor’s Race
description: Ms. Haaland, a former Interior secretary, took a big step toward making history as the first Native American woman to be a governor when she won the primary in her Democratic state.
author: Reis Thebault

title: Remaining Uncounted Ballots in California Elections Are Likely to Lean Democratic
description: Democratic voters returned their ballots later than Republicans in this year’s primaries, which could cause candidate standings to reshuffle as votes continue to be reported.
author: Luke Vrotsos and Christine Zhang

title: A Trump Endorsement Falls Flat: 4 Election Takeaways From Iowa and Beyond
description: The president’s pick for governor of Iowa lost his primary, while Democrats in the state chose their nominee in what they hope will be a competitive Senate race.
author: Reid J. Epstein

title: 2 Hostages Released From Bomb Threat at Chase Bank in Bakersfield, California
description: The police were negotiating with a man inside a Chase Bank branch in Bakersfield, Calif. Two hostages were let go and an unknown number were still being held.
author: Francesca Regalado

title: 5 Takeaways From the California Primary Election
description: Without a dominant candidate in the governor’s race, Democratic voters ultimately wanted to ensure that their party wasn’t shut out of the general election.
author: Laurel Rosenhall and Jennifer Medina

title: Republican support for same-sex marriage has fallen by 18 percentage points since 2022.
author: Ruth Igielnik

title: Scott Wiener and Connie Chan Will Face Off in House Race to Succeed Nancy Pelosi
description: State Sen. Scott Wiener and Supervisor Connie Chan were the top two finishers in the open primary to represent San Francisco as Nancy Pelosi retires.
author: Heather Knight

title: Marni von Wilpert and Jim Desmond Advance in a San Diego-Area House Race
description: Ms. von Wilpert’s win in Tuesday’s open primary eliminated Democrats’ fears that two Republicans would advance to November in the 48th Congressional District.
author: Jennifer Medina

title: 5 Things to Know About the Iowa Senate Candidate Josh Turek
description: A gold medalist on the U.S. men’s wheelchair basketball team, Mr. Turek is running as a “common-sense moderate.”
author: Tim Balk
